   140	@router.post("/{task_id}/add_row", response_model=None)
   141	async def add_row(task_id: str, request: Request) -> HTMLResponse | RedirectResponse:
   142	    """Append one empty proposal row to the draft form.
   143	
   144	    Two transports, same end state:
   145	
   146	    - With JS, htmx posts here with ``HX-Request: true``. We respond
   147	      with the rendered ``_proposal_row.html`` fragment for the new
   148	      row only; htmx swaps it as ``beforeend`` of ``#proposal-rows``
   149	      so the user's existing input is untouched.
   150	    - Without JS, the same button submits the form normally and we
   151	      re-render the whole ``planner_claim.html`` with the
   152	      collected state plus one more empty row.
   153	    """
   154	    session = get_session(request)
   155	    if session is None:
   156	        return RedirectResponse(url="/signin", status_code=303)
   157	    form = await request.form()
   158	    if not csrf_ok(session, form.get("csrf_token")):  # type: ignore[arg-type]
   159	        return _csrf_failure_response()
   160	    if _CLAIMS.get(_claim_key(session.csrf, task_id)) is None:
   161	        return RedirectResponse(
   162	            url="/planner/?banner=claim+missing+from+session",
   163	            status_code=303,
   164	        )
   165	    is_htmx = request.headers.get("hx-request", "").lower() == "true"
   166	
   167	    if is_htmx:
   168	        # The htmx-enhanced path: figure out where the new row goes
   169	        # by counting existing rows in the form, then render only
   170	        # the partial. The user's existing rows are already on the
   171	        # page; htmx appends ours to ``#proposal-rows``.
   172	        existing = max(
   173	            len(form.getlist("slug")),
   174	            len(form.getlist("priority")),
   175	            len(form.getlist("parent_commits")),
   176	            len(form.getlist("rationale")),
   177	        )
   178	        return request.app.state.templates.TemplateResponse(
   179	            request,
   180	            "_proposal_row.html",
   181	            {"i": existing, "row_state": _empty_row(), "row_errs": {}},
   182	        )
   183	
   184	    config = request.app.state.experiment_config
   185	    slugs = [str(v) for v in form.getlist("slug")]
   186	    priorities = [str(v) for v in form.getlist("priority")]
   187	    parents = [str(v) for v in form.getlist("parent_commits")]
   188	    rationales = [str(v) for v in form.getlist("rationale")]
   189	    state = _form_state_from_inputs(slugs, priorities, parents, rationales)
   190	    state.append(_empty_row())
   191	    return request.app.state.templates.TemplateResponse(
   192	        request,
   193	        "planner_claim.html",
   194	        {
   195	            "session": session,
   196	            "task_id": task_id,
   197	            "objective": config.objective,
   198	            "metrics_schema": config.metrics_schema.root,
   199	            "errors": None,
   200	            "form_state": state,
   201	            "row_indices": list(range(len(state))),
   202	        },
   203	    )
   204

   206	@router.post("/{task_id}/submit", response_model=None)
   207	async def submit_plan(task_id: str, request: Request) -> HTMLResponse | RedirectResponse:
   208	    session = get_session(request)
   209	    if session is None:
   210	        return RedirectResponse(url="/signin", status_code=303)
   211	    form = await request.form()
   212	    if not csrf_ok(session, form.get("csrf_token")):  # type: ignore[arg-type]
   213	        return _csrf_failure_response()
   214	    status = form.get("status")
   215	    if status not in ("success", "error"):
   216	        return _bad_request("invalid submit status")
   217	
   218	    token = _CLAIMS.get(_claim_key(session.csrf, task_id))
   219	    if token is None:
   220	        return RedirectResponse(
   221	            url="/planner/?banner=claim+missing+from+session",
   222	            status_code=303,
   223	        )
   224	
   225	    store = request.app.state.store
   226	    config = request.app.state.experiment_config
   227	
   228	    if status == "error":
   229	        try:
   230	            store.submit(task_id, token, PlanSubmission(status="error"))
   231	        except DispatchError as exc:
   232	            return _render_error(request, _wire_error_banner(exc))
   233	        _CLAIMS.pop(_claim_key(session.csrf, task_id), None)
   234	        return _render_submitted(request, task_id, status="error", proposal_ids=())
   235	
   236	    slugs = [str(v) for v in form.getlist("slug")]
   237	    priorities = [str(v) for v in form.getlist("priority")]
   238	    parents = [str(v) for v in form.getlist("parent_commits")]
   239	    rationales = [str(v) for v in form.getlist("rationale")]
   240	    drafts, errors = parse_proposal_rows(slugs, priorities, parents, rationales)
   241	    if errors:
   242	        return request.app.state.templates.TemplateResponse(
   243	            request,
   244	            "planner_claim.html",
   245	            {
   246	                "session": session,
   247	                "task_id": task_id,
   248	                "objective": config.objective,
   249	                "metrics_schema": config.metrics_schema.root,
   250	                "errors": errors,
   251	                "form_state": _form_state_from_inputs(slugs, priorities, parents, rationales),
   252	                "row_indices": list(range(max(1, len(slugs) or 1))),
   253	            },
   254	            status_code=400,
   255	        )
   256	
   257	    proposal_ids: list[str] = []
   258	    artifacts_dir = request.app.state.artifacts_dir
   259	    now: Callable[[], Any] = request.app.state.now
   260	
   261	    # Phase 1: write artifacts + create proposals as drafting.
   262	    for draft in drafts:
   263	        proposal_id = uuid.uuid4().hex
   264	        artifacts_uri = write_proposal_artifact(
   265	            artifacts_dir, proposal_id, draft.rationale
   266	        )
   267	        proposal = _make_proposal(
   268	            proposal_id=proposal_id,
   269	            experiment_id=request.app.state.experiment_id,
   270	            draft=draft,
   271	            artifacts_uri=artifacts_uri,
   272	            now_iso=_iso(now()),
   273	        )
   274	        try:
   275	            store.create_proposal(proposal)
   276	        except DispatchError as exc:
   277	            return _render_error(
   278	                request,
   279	                f"create_proposal failed for {proposal_id}: {_wire_error_banner(exc)}",
   280	            )
   281	        proposal_ids.append(proposal_id)
   282	
   283	    # Phase 2: flip to ready.
   284	    for proposal_id in proposal_ids:
   285	        try:
   286	            store.mark_proposal_ready(proposal_id)
   287	        except DispatchError as exc:
   288	            return _render_error(
   289	                request,
   290	                f"mark_proposal_ready failed for {proposal_id}: {_wire_error_banner(exc)}",
   291	            )
   292	
   293	    # Phase 3: submit, with retry-before-orphan.
   294	    submission = PlanSubmission(status="success", proposal_ids=tuple(proposal_ids))
   295	    ok, banner = _retry_submit(store, task_id, token, submission)
   296	    if not ok:
   297	        return _render_orphaned(request, task_id, proposal_ids, banner=banner)
   298	
   299	    _CLAIMS.pop(_claim_key(session.csrf, task_id), None)
   300	    return _render_submitted(
   301	        request, task_id, status="success", proposal_ids=tuple(proposal_ids)
   302	    )

   308	def _retry_submit(
   309	    store: Any, task_id: str, token: str, submission: PlanSubmission
   310	) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:
   311	    """Resubmit on transport-only failures up to 3 times.
   312	
   313	    Per chapter 07 §2.4 / §8.1, a content-equivalent resubmit is
   314	    idempotent. Returns ``(ok, banner)``:
   315	
   316	    - ``(True, None)`` on success (including a same-content
   317	      resubmit that returns 200).
   318	    - ``(False, banner)`` on a definitive divergent response or
   319	      after 3 indeterminate failures. ``banner`` is the canonical
   320	      ``eden://error/<name>`` (or a transport summary) suitable for

   276	class TestDefinitiveSubmitErrors:
   277	    """Each chapter-07 §7 error name surfaces on the orphan page.
   278	
   279	    Note on operational semantics: by the time Phase 3 submit runs,
   280	    Phase 1 has already created proposals (drafting) and Phase 2 has
   281	    marked them ready. A definitive wire error at submit means those
   282	    proposals are orphaned — the form-input preservation that
   283	    applies to *validation* errors does not apply here, because the
   284	    work product (the proposals themselves) is what needs recovery,
   285	    not the form inputs. The orphan page lists the orphaned
   286	    proposal IDs and surfaces the canonical error type so an
   287	    operator can decide whether to reclaim the plan task or
   288	    garbage-collect.
   289	    """
   290	
   291	    def _setup_claim(
   292	        self, client: TestClient, store: InMemoryStore, task_id: str
   293	    ) -> str:
   294	        store.create_plan_task(task_id)
   295	        token = get_csrf(client)
   296	        resp = client.post(
   297	            f"/planner/{task_id}/claim",
   298	            data={"csrf_token": token},
   299	            follow_redirects=False,
   300	        )
   301	        assert resp.status_code == 303
   302	        return token
   303	
   304	    def _submit(
   305	        self, client: TestClient, task_id: str, csrf: str
   306	    ):
   307	        return client.post(
   308	            f"/planner/{task_id}/submit",
   309	            data={
   310	                "csrf_token": csrf,
   311	                "status": "success",
   312	                "slug": "feat-x",
   313	                "priority": "1.0",
   314	                "parent_commits": "a" * 40,
   315	                "rationale": "rationale",
   316	            },
   317	        )

   319	    def test_wrong_token_lands_on_orphan_with_banner(
   320	        self,
   321	        signed_in_client: TestClient,
   322	        store: InMemoryStore,
   323	        monkeypatch,
   324	    ) -> None:
   325	        from eden_storage import WrongToken

item.text: I’ve narrowed the implementation surface. Now I’m checking the route-level tests and a couple of direct request cases that the commit didn’t add explicitly, so the review can distinguish “covered elsewhere” from “not covered at all.”

item.aggregated_output:
1	# eden-web-ui
     2	
     3	Reference Web UI service for the EDEN protocol. Phase 9 chunk 1
     4	delivers the UI shell plus the planner module — enough surface for a
     5	human to play one role end-to-end through a browser.
     6	
     7	The UI service is a **backend-for-frontend (BFF)**: it holds the
     8	`--shared-token` (the chapter 07 §12 reference bearer the
     9	orchestrator and worker hosts already use), runs `eden_wire.StoreClient`
    10	in-process to talk to the task-store-server, and exposes only
    11	server-rendered HTML to the browser. The browser never sees the
    12	shared token; it gets a signed session cookie.
    13	
    14	Rendering uses Jinja2 templates with [HTMX](https://htmx.org/) as a
    15	progressive-enhancement layer. Every mutating route works without
    16	JS (plain form-POST + 303-redirect / re-render); HTMX-aware routes
    17	additionally return a fragment when the browser sends
    18	`HX-Request: true`. The chunk-1 example is "add another proposal
    19	row" — HTMX appends one new row inline; without JS the same
    20	button does a full-page re-render. HTMX is vendored at
    21	`src/eden_web_ui/static/htmx-1.9.12.min.js`
    22	(SHA-256 `449317ade7881e949510db614991e195c3a099c4c791c24dacec55f9f4a2a452`)
    23	so the UI works offline and in CI without external network.
    24	
    25	## Run locally
    26	
    27	```bash
    28	python3 -m eden_task_store_server \
    29	    --db-path /tmp/eden.sqlite \
    30	    --experiment-id exp-1 \
    31	    --experiment-config tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml \
    32	    --port 0 \
    33	    --shared-token devtoken \
    34	    &
    35	
    36	# (Read EDEN_TASK_STORE_LISTENING from stdout to learn the port.)
    37	
    38	python3 -m eden_web_ui \
    39	    --task-store-url http://127.0.0.1:<port> \
    40	    --experiment-id exp-1 \
    41	    --experiment-config tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml \
    42	    --shared-token devtoken \
    43	    --session-secret "$(openssl rand -hex 32)" \
    44	    --artifacts-dir /tmp/eden-artifacts \
    45	    --port 0
    46	```
    47	
    48	The web-ui announces `EDEN_WEB_UI_LISTENING host=... port=...` on stdout
    49	on bind so harnesses (and the test suite) can discover the ephemeral
    50	port without scraping logs.
    51	
    52	## Auth model
    53	
    54	- The session cookie holds `{worker_id, csrf}` and is signed with
    55	  `--session-secret` via `itsdangerous`.
    56	- Cookie attributes: `HttpOnly`, `SameSite=Lax`, `Path=/`. `Secure`
    57	  is opt-in via `--secure-cookies` (use behind TLS).
    58	- Every mutating route validates a `csrf_token` form field in
    59	  constant time. The cookie's `SameSite=Lax` is **not** treated as
    60	  sufficient on its own.
    61	- The shared bearer never reaches the browser, the rendered HTML,
    62	  any session cookie, or any structured log line.
    63	
    64	## Stranded-claim recovery
    65	
    66	Every UI claim sets `expires_at = now + --claim-ttl-seconds`
    67	(default 1 hour). The orchestrator service runs
    68	`eden_dispatch.sweep_expired_claims` once per iteration so claims
    69	abandoned by closing the tab are reclaimed automatically — no
    70	operator action required.
    71	
    72	## Planner submit flow
    73	
    74	The planner module pins three phases:
    75	
    76	1. **Phase 1 — drafting.** For every proposal: write rationale
    77	   markdown to `<artifacts-dir>/<proposal_id>.md` (atomically, via
    78	   tmp-and-rename), build a `file://` URI, then call
    79	   `store.create_proposal(state="drafting")`. Drafting proposals
    80	   are invisible to the orchestrator's dispatch path.
    81	2. **Phase 2 — ready.** Loop over the just-created proposals and
    82	   call `store.mark_proposal_ready` for each.
    83	3. **Phase 3 — submit.** `store.submit(...)` with retry-before-orphan
    84	   on transport-shaped failures (3 attempts, exponential backoff,
    85	   leveraging chapter 07 §2.4 / §8.1 idempotent resubmit). On a
    86	   definitive divergent response or after the retries are
    87	   exhausted, the orphaned-proposals error page lists the
    88	   `ready`-but-unreferenced proposal IDs for operator recovery.
    89	
    90	The narrowest unsafe window is between Phase 2 and Phase 3:
    91	proposals are `ready` but not yet referenced by a submitted plan
    92	task. We accept this for the reference impl; it applies equally
    93	to the existing scripted planner host. A spec-level fix (atomic
    94	ready-and-submit) is out of scope for chunk 1.
    95	
    96	## What this chunk does **not** ship
    97	
    98	- Implementer / evaluator role modules (9c / 9d).
    99	- Observability views, admin-reclaim button (9e).
   100	- Multi-experiment switcher (Phase 12).
   101	- Per-user authentication (Milestone 3).
   102	- Compose / Dockerization (Phase 10).

AGENTS.md:19:**Phase 9 chunk 1 complete.** A reference Web UI service ([`reference/services/web-ui/`](reference/services/web-ui/)) ships the UI shell + planner module. It is a backend-for-frontend over `eden_wire.StoreClient`: holds the shared bearer, talks to the task-store-server only via the wire binding, and renders server-side Jinja templates. HTMX 1.9.12 is vendored under `static/` (SHA-256 pinned in the service README) as a progressive-enhancement layer — every mutating route works without JS via plain form-POST + 303 redirect / re-render, and HTMX-aware routes also serve a fragment when the browser sends `HX-Request: true` (the chunk-1 example is "add another proposal row" inline). The session cookie is `itsdangerous`-signed (HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax + Path=/, plus opt-in `--secure-cookies` for TLS deployments) and carries a per-session CSRF token validated on every mutating route. The planner page surfaces the experiment's `objective` and `metrics_schema` (read-only) plus a recent-proposals/recent-trials context panel, then walks a 3-phase write — `create_proposal(state="drafting")` for every row, then `mark_proposal_ready` for each, then `submit` with retry-before-orphan (3 attempts, exponential backoff) leveraging chapter 07 §2.4 / §8.1 idempotent resubmit. The narrowest unsafe window (Phase 2 → Phase 3 ready-but-unreferenced proposals) is documented as a known limitation that applies equally to the existing scripted planner host. Cross-cutting addition: `eden_dispatch.sweep_expired_claims` runs once per `run_orchestrator_loop` iteration so UI claims with `expires_at` are reclaimed automatically when an abandoned tab strands a task. Tests cover unit (route-by-route + CSRF), cross-request flow (claim → submit + validation recovery + stranded-claim recovery via the sweeper), security invariants (cookie attributes + bearer non-leak), partial-write recovery (Phase-2 mid-loop failure leaves drafting-only; Phase-3 retry-then-orphan), and a two-process `pytest.mark.e2e` test that drives the full planner flow over real HTTP against a real task-store-server subprocess.

item.text: The cross-request tests pass location is different than I first assumed; I’m correcting that now and reading the route/security test files that already cover generic CSRF/session behavior, because that affects whether the HTMX path has a real gap or just an unlabelled one.

item.aggregated_output:
1	"""Pin the security invariants codex flagged in plan review.
     2	
     3	- Cookie attributes (HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, Path=/, Secure iff
     4	  --secure-cookies).
     5	- The shared bearer never appears in any rendered HTML or
     6	  Set-Cookie header.
     7	- CSRF token is per-session; mismatch returns 403.
     8	"""
     9	
    10	from __future__ import annotations
    11	
    12	import logging
    13	from pathlib import Path
    14	
    15	from conftest import (
    16	    EXPERIMENT_ID,
    17	    SESSION_SECRET,
    18	    SHARED_TOKEN,
    19	    WORKER_ID,
    20	    _config,
    21	    _now,
    22	    get_csrf,
    23	)
    24	from eden_storage import InMemoryStore
    25	from eden_web_ui import make_app
    26	from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
    27	
    28	
    29	class TestCookieAttributes:
    30	    def test_default_secure_off(self, signed_in_client: TestClient) -> None:
    31	        # Set-cookie attributes appear on the *response* — easier to assert
    32	        # by re-issuing signin and reading the header.
    33	        resp = signed_in_client.post("/signin", follow_redirects=False)
    34	        header = resp.headers.get("set-cookie", "")
    35	        assert "HttpOnly" in header
    36	        assert "SameSite=lax" in header
    37	        assert "Secure" not in header
    38	        assert "Path=/" in header
    39	
    40	    def test_secure_cookies_true_sets_secure(
    41	        self, store: InMemoryStore, artifacts_dir: Path
    42	    ) -> None:
    43	        app = make_app(
    44	            store=store,
    45	            experiment_id=EXPERIMENT_ID,
    46	            experiment_config=_config(),
    47	            worker_id=WORKER_ID,
    48	            session_secret=SESSION_SECRET,
    49	            claim_ttl_seconds=3600,
    50	            artifacts_dir=artifacts_dir,
    51	            secure_cookies=True,
    52	            now=_now,
    53	        )
    54	        with TestClient(app) as c:
    55	            resp = c.post("/signin", follow_redirects=False)
    56	            assert "Secure" in resp.headers["set-cookie"]
    57	
    58	
    59	class TestBearerLeak:
    60	    def test_bearer_not_in_rendered_html_or_cookies(
    61	        self,
    62	        store: InMemoryStore,
    63	        artifacts_dir: Path,
    64	        caplog,
    65	    ) -> None:
    66	        """Even though we don't pass the bearer to make_app today (the
    67	        StoreClient holds it; tests use InMemoryStore), assert that no
    68	        endpoint renders or echoes the configured bearer string."""
    69	        app = make_app(
    70	            store=store,
    71	            experiment_id=EXPERIMENT_ID,
    72	            experiment_config=_config(),
    73	            worker_id=WORKER_ID,
    74	            session_secret=SESSION_SECRET,
    75	            claim_ttl_seconds=3600,
    76	            artifacts_dir=artifacts_dir,

52	## Tech stack — decision and alternatives considered
    53	
    54	**Chosen stack:** FastAPI + Jinja2 + vendored HTMX 1.9.12, plus a
    55	single hand-written CSS file. The UI service is a separate process
    56	under `reference/services/web-ui/` that acts as a
    57	**backend-for-frontend (BFF)**: it holds the `--shared-token` (the
    58	same reference-bearer the orchestrator and worker hosts already
    59	use), runs `eden_wire.StoreClient` in-process to talk to the
    60	task-store-server, and exposes only server-rendered HTML routes to
    61	the browser. The browser never sees the shared token; it gets a
    62	session cookie signed with a server-side secret.
    63	
    64	This BFF split is a property of the architecture, not the rendering
    65	choice — the same split could host an SPA, a Phoenix-LiveView-style
    66	push UI, or plain form-POST navigation. HTMX is included as a
    67	**progressive-enhancement layer**: every mutating route works
    68	without JS via plain form-POST + 303-redirect / re-render, and
    69	HTMX-aware routes additionally serve a smaller fragment when the
    70	browser sends `HX-Request: true`. The decisive use case in chunk 1
    71	is the "add another proposal row" interaction, where HTMX swaps in
    72	a fresh row inline (`hx-target="#proposal-rows" hx-swap="beforeend"`)
    73	instead of full-page-reloading a form the user is partway through.
    74	9c (implementer), 9d (evaluator), and especially 9e (observability
    75	views) earn HTMX's cost much more clearly; landing it now means
    76	those chunks don't have to retrofit it.
    77	
    78	HTMX is **vendored** at
    79	`src/eden_web_ui/static/htmx-1.9.12.min.js` (SHA-256
    80	`449317ade7881e949510db614991e195c3a099c4c791c24dacec55f9f4a2a452`)
    81	so the UI works offline and in CI without external network.
    82	
    83	### Why this stack
    84	
    85	1. **No new toolchain.** FastAPI, Jinja2, and httpx are already direct
    86	   or transitive dependencies of `eden-wire` and the existing services.
    87	   HTMX is a single CDN-hosted JS file (or vendored under `static/`).
    88	   No Node, no Vite, no npm lockfile in a Python-first repo.
    89	2. **One language end-to-end.** Server-side rendering keeps planner /
    90	   implementer / evaluator forms in Python, where the typed
    91	   `PlanSubmission` / `ImplementSubmission` / `EvaluateSubmission`
    92	   payloads they construct already live (`eden_storage.submissions`).
    93	   No duplicate validation logic in a JS layer.
    94	3. **`StoreClient` already satisfies the `Store` Protocol.** The UI
    95	   service consumes the same surface the orchestrator and worker
    96	   hosts use. We don't add a second client implementation; we don't
    97	   expose the wire protocol directly to the browser.
    98	4. **Reference-impl ethos.** The repo's job is to demonstrate one
    99	   conforming implementation, readable to someone evaluating whether
   100	   to build a different one. An SPA buries the role-contract logic
   101	   behind JSON-over-fetch plumbing; server-rendered forms keep the
   102	   spec-to-code mapping legible.
   103	5. **Auth surface stays small.** The browser holds a session cookie
   104	   only; it never gets the shared bearer. The threat model is
   105	   deliberately spelled out in §B (a leaked cookie can act as the
   106	   named worker_id through the UI but cannot bypass the UI service
   107	   to talk to the task-store directly); the goal here is to keep
   108	   the wire-protocol auth surface unchanged, not to claim the
   109	   cookie is unforgeable.
   110	
   111	### Alternatives considered
   112	
   113	#### A. BFF-backed SPA (React / Vue / Svelte talking to the UI service's JSON API)
   114	
   115	- **Pros:** Richer interactions; separates view logic from server
   116	  state cleanly; idiomatic for production webapps; the BFF split
   117	  preserves the shared-bearer-stays-server-side property.
   118	- **Cons:**
   119	  - Adds a Node toolchain, a build step, and a JS lockfile to a repo
   120	    whose every other artifact is Python or Markdown. Doubles the CI
   121	    matrix (frontend lint / build / test alongside `uv run …`).
   122	  - Duplicates validation: form fields validated client-side, then
   123	    re-validated server-side, then re-validated by the wire schemas.
   124	    Three sources of truth for "is this submission well-formed."
   125	  - The role-contract logic — "for plan submission, create N proposals,
   126	    mark them ready, submit with their ids" — moves into JS, away
   127	    from where `PlanSubmission` is defined.
   128	- **Verdict:** Defensible if a richer client experience is the
   129	  primary goal. For a reference impl whose job is to make the
   130	  spec-to-code mapping legible, the costs outweigh the benefits.
   131	
   132	#### A'. Browser-direct SPA (no BFF; browser holds the shared bearer)
   133	
   134	Listed separately because it's the failure mode of a careless SPA
   135	choice, not a defensible alternative on its own.
   136	
   137	- **Cons:** Exposes the chapter 07 §12 shared bearer to the browser;
   138	  forces a CORS story; deletes the wire/UI auth boundary the BFF
   139	  preserves.
   140	- **Verdict:** Explicitly out — included only to flag that picking
   141	  "SPA" without the BFF is strictly worse than either A or the
   142	  chosen stack.
   143	
   144	#### B. Plain server-rendered Jinja with progressive enhancement (no HTMX)
   145	
   146	This is the closest neighbor to the chosen stack.
   147	
   148	- **Pros:** Exactly the same dependency graph minus 14 KB of JS;
   149	  forms degrade to full-page POST + 303 redirect on every action;
   150	  no client-side state to reason about; trivially testable with a
   151	  plain HTTP client.
   152	- **Cons:**
   153	  - Claim-then-show-form needs either a server-side redirect or a
   154	    two-page flow; not a deal-breaker but it's clunkier.
   155	  - Any partial update — e.g. "you claimed task X, now this row
   156	    moves out of pending" — either reflows the whole page or
   157	    requires hand-rolled `fetch` + `outerHTML` swaps. HTMX
   158	    automates exactly that with `hx-target` / `hx-swap`.
   159	- **Verdict:** Live alternative, not a strawman. We pick HTMX for
   160	  the partial-update ergonomics it gives the planner-list →
   161	  claim-form transition; if HTMX adds friction during impl, the
   162	  fallback is this option, not a heavier framework.
   163	
   164	#### C. Plain HTML + vanilla `fetch` + manual DOM replacement
   165	
   166	- **Pros:** No JS dependency at all.
   167	- **Cons:** Reimplements HTMX badly. Every form ends up reinventing
   168	  the same `fetch → response.text() → element.outerHTML = …`
   169	  plumbing. The bug surface and code volume both grow per route.
   170	- **Verdict:** Strictly dominated by option B (drop the JS entirely
   171	  and reload) or the chosen stack (use HTMX for the swap).
   172	
   173	#### D. Phoenix-LiveView-style server-pushed UI (e.g. via SSE/WebSocket)
   174	
   175	- **Pros:** "Live" feel without a JS framework; great fit for the
   176	  observability views in 9e.
   177	- **Cons:** Phase 9 chunk 1 is request-response by scope. Pulling
   178	  the live-update transport in now adds complexity (subscribe
   179	  endpoint, reconnect logic) without a place to use it; a stranded
   180	  human claim doesn't push, it times out. Revisit specifically for

712	  manual `grep -r "$EDEN_SHARED_TOKEN"` verification step. A
   713	  reviewer should still spot-check any new template that
   714	  receives the configuration object.
   715	- **`itsdangerous` session-secret rotation.** Out of scope for this
   716	  chunk; restarting the service with a different `--session-secret`
   717	  invalidates outstanding sessions, which is the documented
   718	  behavior. A real auth phase replaces this anyway.
   719	- **Vendored HTMX bit-rot.** Pin the version in `pyproject.toml`
   720	  comments and check the SHA-256 in `web-ui/README.md`; bumping is a
   721	  one-line follow-up but not silent.
   722	- **HTMX surprises.** If a partial-update interaction turns out to
   723	  need a feature HTMX doesn't cleanly support, fall back to
   724	  full-page submits for that flow rather than reaching for a JS
   725	  framework. The CSS is already a single file; the templates degrade
   726	  gracefully.
   727	- **Test scope creep.** The existing Phase 8b e2e test takes ~3
   728	  seconds because it forks five processes. Adding a second e2e test
   729	  that forks two more (task-store + UI) keeps it on the order of
   730	  another ~3 seconds; this is acceptable. If e2e total exceeds
   731	  ~30 seconds we revisit consolidating, but not preemptively.
   732	- **Reviewers' default expectation of a SPA.** The "Tech stack —
   733	  decision and alternatives considered" section is deliberately
   734	  detailed precisely because reviewers may default to "modern means
   735	  React." The codex-review should specifically scrutinize that
   736	  decision.

300	  `<id>.md.tmp`, rename to `<id>.md`) and returns the
   301	  `file://<absolute path>` URI.
   302	- `src/eden_web_ui/templates/base.html` — shell layout, navigation,
   303	  HTMX include, current-experiment banner.
   304	- `src/eden_web_ui/templates/index.html` — landing page.
   305	- `src/eden_web_ui/templates/planner_list.html` — pending plan tasks.
   306	- `src/eden_web_ui/templates/planner_claim.html` — post-claim draft
   307	  form, full-page render after a `POST /planner/{id}/claim` 303
   308	  redirect.
   309	- `src/eden_web_ui/templates/planner_submitted.html` — success
   310	  confirmation fragment.
   311	- `src/eden_web_ui/templates/_error.html` — generic error fragment.
   312	- `src/eden_web_ui/static/style.css` — single hand-written stylesheet,
   313	  served by FastAPI's `StaticFiles`.
   314	- `src/eden_web_ui/static/htmx-1.9.12.min.js` — vendored HTMX
   315	  (SHA-256 pinned in the README) so the UI works offline / in CI
   316	  without external network.
   317	- `src/eden_web_ui/templates/_proposal_row.html` — single proposal
   318	  row; rendered standalone for HTMX `beforeend` swaps and inline
   319	  by `planner_claim.html` for the no-JS fallback.
   320	- `tests/__init__.py`
   321	- `tests/test_app.py` — `TestClient`-driven unit tests against
   322	  `make_app(store=InMemoryStore(...), …)`. Covers each route's
   323	  rendering and validation in isolation: GET / returns 200, GET
   324	  /planner lists pending plan tasks, malformed POST returns 400,
   325	  CSRF-token mismatch returns 400.
   326	- `tests/test_planner_flow.py` — covers the **cross-request flow**
   327	  that `test_app.py` deliberately does not exercise: full
   328	  claim → draft → submit happy path, expired-claim-token recovery
   329	  (re-render with banner, input preserved), invalid-CSRF-token
   330	  rejection on each mutating route, mid-flow `create_proposal`
   331	  HTTP failure leaves only `drafting` proposals (asserted via
   332	  `store.list_proposals(state="ready")` returning empty), mid-flow
   333	  `submit` failure surfaces orphaned proposal IDs to the user.
   334	  Driven by `httpx.AsyncClient` against the real ASGI app so
   335	  cookie/session round-tripping is authentic.
   336	- `tests/test_security_invariants.py` — focused unit tests that
   337	  pin the security guarantees codex flagged:
   338	  - The shared bearer never appears in any rendered HTML, any
   339	    JSON-line log captured during a test request, or any
   340	    `Set-Cookie` header.

16	
    17	## Current phase
    18	
    19	**Phase 9 chunk 1 complete.** A reference Web UI service ([`reference/services/web-ui/`](reference/services/web-ui/)) ships the UI shell + planner module. It is a backend-for-frontend over `eden_wire.StoreClient`: holds the shared bearer, talks to the task-store-server only via the wire binding, and renders server-side Jinja templates. HTMX 1.9.12 is vendored under `static/` (SHA-256 pinned in the service README) as a progressive-enhancement layer — every mutating route works without JS via plain form-POST + 303 redirect / re-render, and HTMX-aware routes also serve a fragment when the browser sends `HX-Request: true` (the chunk-1 example is "add another proposal row" inline). The session cookie is `itsdangerous`-signed (HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax + Path=/, plus opt-in `--secure-cookies` for TLS deployments) and carries a per-session CSRF token validated on every mutating route. The planner page surfaces the experiment's `objective` and `metrics_schema` (read-only) plus a recent-proposals/recent-trials context panel, then walks a 3-phase write — `create_proposal(state="drafting")` for every row, then `mark_proposal_ready` for each, then `submit` with retry-before-orphan (3 attempts, exponential backoff) leveraging chapter 07 §2.4 / §8.1 idempotent resubmit. The narrowest unsafe window (Phase 2 → Phase 3 ready-but-unreferenced proposals) is documented as a known limitation that applies equally to the existing scripted planner host. Cross-cutting addition: `eden_dispatch.sweep_expired_claims` runs once per `run_orchestrator_loop` iteration so UI claims with `expires_at` are reclaimed automatically when an abandoned tab strands a task. Tests cover unit (route-by-route + CSRF), cross-request flow (claim → submit + validation recovery + stranded-claim recovery via the sweeper), security invariants (cookie attributes + bearer non-leak), partial-write recovery (Phase-2 mid-loop failure leaves drafting-only; Phase-3 retry-then-orphan), and a two-process `pytest.mark.e2e` test that drives the full planner flow over real HTTP against a real task-store-server subprocess.
    20	
    21	**Phase 8c complete.** The in-process dispatch driver is removed: `eden-dispatch.run_experiment` was deleted and its public-API surface narrows to `run_orchestrator_iteration` plus the scripted workers (`ScriptedPlanner` / `ScriptedImplementer` / `ScriptedEvaluator`). The Phase 5 / 7b in-process end-to-end tests in `reference/packages/eden-dispatch/tests/test_end_to_end.py` were deleted; the lifecycle-reconstruction invariant they covered moved into `reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py` as a `_reconstruct_lifecycle` fold over the post-teardown `SqliteStore.read_range()`. The malformed-success → `validation_error` routing test in `eden-storage/tests/test_store_hardening.py` was rewritten to drive `run_orchestrator_iteration` directly through the legitimate `Store` API. The `pytest.mark.e2e` real-subprocess test from Phase 8b is now the only end-to-end coverage path; components communicate exclusively through the Phase 8a wire binding.
    22	
    23	**Phase 8b complete.** Five reference services under [`reference/services/`](reference/services/) extract the dispatch loop into independent OS processes that communicate only over the Phase 8a wire binding: [`task-store-server/`](reference/services/task-store-server/) hosts the `Store` behind uvicorn; [`orchestrator/`](reference/services/orchestrator/) runs the finalize/dispatch/integrate half of the loop against a `StoreClient`; [`planner/`](reference/services/planner/), [`implementer/`](reference/services/implementer/), and [`evaluator/`](reference/services/evaluator/) are standalone worker hosts. A new shared package [`reference/services/_common/`](reference/services/_common/) holds the JSON-line logger, SIGTERM/SIGINT stop flag, readiness probe, common argparse flags, scripted `plan_fn`/`implement_fn`/`evaluate_fn` profiles, and the `seed_bare_repo` helper. `eden-dispatch` exposes `run_orchestrator_iteration` as its orchestrator-side entry point so the standalone orchestrator drives only that half (the in-process `run_experiment` driver remained alongside it through 8b and was deleted in 8c). `eden-wire.make_app(store, *, shared_token=…)` and `eden-wire.StoreClient(…, token=…)` add reference-only bearer-token auth: requests without the right `Authorization: Bearer <token>` are rejected as `eden://reference-error/unauthorized` (HTTP 401), under a new namespace deliberately kept out of the normative `eden://error/…` vocabulary in [`spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md`](spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md) §7. A new informative §12 in chapter 07 documents the scheme. The implementer host writes real git commits (single-parent or merge, honoring `proposal.parent_commits`) on `work/*` branches in the bare repo the orchestrator integrates from. A `pytest.mark.e2e` test in [`reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py`](reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py) forks all five processes on an ephemeral port, drives a 3-trial experiment to quiescence over real HTTP + real SQLite + real git, and tears down with SIGTERM → SIGKILL fallback.
    24	
    25	**Phase 8a complete.** `eden-wire` ships an HTTP binding for the chapter 4 / 5 / 6 §3.4 / 8 §§1.1–2.1 operations specified in the new [`spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md`](spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md) chapter. The package exposes a FastAPI ``make_app(store)`` that routes every wire endpoint to a ``Store`` instance, and a ``StoreClient`` that satisfies the same ``Store`` Protocol against the HTTP surface — so existing callers (dispatch driver, integrator) work across the process boundary unchanged. Errors round-trip as RFC 7807 problem+json with a closed vocabulary of ``eden://error/<name>`` types (§7). ``Store.integrate_trial`` is now same-value idempotent (§5 of the chapter); the ``Integrator`` distinguishes different-SHA divergence (``AtomicityViolation``, no ref compensation) from other synchronous rejections (normal §3.4 compensating-delete flow). ``StoreClient.integrate_trial`` reconciles transport-indeterminate failures via read-back: the three outcomes are confirmed success, confirmed divergence (``InvalidPrecondition``), or ``IndeterminateIntegration`` when the server's outcome cannot be determined. Polling + long-poll-style subscribe are both bound (§6). Worker-process extraction, SSE/WebSocket push, and cut-over of in-process paths remain Phase 8b / 8c.
    26	
    27	**Phase 6 complete.** The `eden-storage` package ships the `Store` structural interface for the task store, event log, and proposal/trial persistence sides of chapter 8 — collapsed into a single Protocol per §7 implementation latitude. Two conforming backends satisfy it: `InMemoryStore` (fast, non-durable; moved from `eden-dispatch`) and `SqliteStore` (durable across restarts via a WAL-mode SQLite database with `synchronous=FULL`, matching §3.1's crash-survival requirement). The Protocol covers the spec-literal `create_task` / `replay` / `read_range` operations alongside the typed convenience helpers. Both backends share the transition logic in `_base.py`, and the same conformance scenarios are parametrized across both — drift from the Protocol surfaces in tests, not in production. Restart-safety tests close and reopen a SQLite store mid-experiment to confirm state, event log, and claim tokens survive, and a monkey-patched `_apply_commit` failure verifies rollback. The artifact store (§5), `subscribe` streaming (§2.1), and Postgres remain non-goals for Phase 6; they land in Phase 10 / Phase 8 / later respectively.
    28

1	"""Typed form parsers for the planner module.
     2	
     3	Form input arrives as a list of repeated field rows (one per
     4	proposal). We parse it into a list of ``ProposalDraft`` objects
     5	plus the planner-level status. Validation errors are accumulated
     6	field-by-field so the form re-renders with the user's input intact.
     7	"""
     8	
     9	from __future__ import annotations
    10	
    11	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    12	
    13	
    14	@dataclass(frozen=True)
    15	class ProposalDraft:
    16	    """Validated planner-form input for one proposal.
    17	
    18	    The canonical ``Proposal`` model is constructed by the route
    19	    handler from this draft plus the server-generated
    20	    ``proposal_id`` and ``artifacts_uri`` from the artifact writer.
    21	    """
    22	
    23	    slug: str
    24	    priority: float
    25	    parent_commits: tuple[str, ...]
    26	    rationale: str
    27	
    28	
    29	@dataclass
    30	class FormErrors:
    31	    """Accumulated per-field error messages, indexed by row then field."""
    32	
    33	    by_row: dict[int, dict[str, str]] = field(default_factory=dict)
    34	    overall: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    35	
    36	    def add(self, row: int, field_name: str, message: str) -> None:
    37	        """Record a field-level error for ``row`` / ``field_name``."""
    38	        self.by_row.setdefault(row, {})[field_name] = message
    39	
    40	    def add_overall(self, message: str) -> None:
    41	        """Record a form-level (not row-specific) error."""
    42	        self.overall.append(message)
    43	
    44	    def __bool__(self) -> bool:
    45	        return bool(self.by_row) or bool(self.overall)
    46	
    47	
    48	def parse_proposal_rows(
    49	    slugs: list[str],
    50	    priorities: list[str],
    51	    parent_commits_csv: list[str],
    52	    rationales: list[str],
    53	) -> tuple[list[ProposalDraft], FormErrors]:
    54	    """Parse parallel-list form input into validated drafts + accumulated errors.
    55	
    56	    Each row is one proposal. Fields are validated independently so a
    57	    bad row 1 still yields an error report covering rows 2..N.
    58	    """
    59	    errors = FormErrors()
    60	    n = max(len(slugs), len(priorities), len(parent_commits_csv), len(rationales))
    61	    if n == 0:
    62	        errors.add_overall("at least one proposal row is required")
    63	        return [], errors
    64	
    65	    drafts: list[ProposalDraft] = []
    66	    parsed_count = 0
    67	    for i in range(n):
    68	        slug = (slugs[i] if i < len(slugs) else "").strip()
    69	        priority_raw = (priorities[i] if i < len(priorities) else "").strip()
    70	        parents_raw = (
    71	            parent_commits_csv[i] if i < len(parent_commits_csv) else ""
    72	        ).strip()
    73	        rationale = (rationales[i] if i < len(rationales) else "").strip()
    74	
    75	        # Skip fully-empty rows (priority defaults to "1.0" so a true
    76	        # empty row has slug+parents+rationale all blank). The "add
    77	        # another row" path adds blank trailing rows; the user
    78	        # shouldn't be forced to fill every one before submitting.
    79	        if not slug and not parents_raw and not rationale:
    80	            continue
    81	
    82	        if not slug:
    83	            errors.add(i, "slug", "slug is required")
    84	        elif not all(c.isalnum() or c in "-_" for c in slug):
    85	            errors.add(i, "slug", "slug must be alphanumeric / dash / underscore")
    86	
    87	        try:
    88	            priority = float(priority_raw)
    89	        except ValueError:
    90	            errors.add(i, "priority", "priority must be a number")
    91	            priority = 0.0
    92	
    93	        parents = tuple(p.strip() for p in parents_raw.split(",") if p.strip())
    94	        if not parents:
    95	            errors.add(i, "parent_commits", "at least one parent commit SHA is required")
    96	        else:
    97	            for parent in parents:
    98	                if not (len(parent) == 40 and all(c in "0123456789abcdef" for c in parent.lower())):
    99	                    errors.add(i, "parent_commits", f"invalid SHA: {parent!r}")
   100	                    break
   101	
   102	        if not rationale:
   103	            errors.add(i, "rationale", "rationale markdown is required")
   104	
   105	        parsed_count += 1
   106	        if not errors.by_row.get(i):
   107	            drafts.append(
   108	                ProposalDraft(
   109	                    slug=slug,
   110	                    priority=priority,
   111	                    parent_commits=parents,
   112	                    rationale=rationale,
   113	                )
   114	            )
   115	
   116	    if parsed_count == 0:
   117	        errors.add_overall("at least one proposal row must be filled in")
   118	
   119	    return drafts, errors

    52	    ``now`` is injected so tests can pin time deterministically; the
    53	    CLI passes a real wall-clock factory.
    54	    """
    55	    app = FastAPI(title="EDEN reference Web UI", version="0.0.1")
    56	    app.mount(
    57	        "/static",
    58	        StaticFiles(directory=str(_STATIC_DIR)),

    61	    templates = Jinja2Templates(directory=str(_TEMPLATES_DIR))

    68	    app.state.session_codec = SessionCodec(session_secret)
    69	    app.state.claim_ttl_seconds = claim_ttl_seconds
    70	    app.state.artifacts_dir = Path(artifacts_dir)
    71	    app.state.secure_cookies = secure_cookies

from pathlib import Path
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path('reference/services/web-ui/tests').resolve()))
from conftest import _config, _now, EXPERIMENT_ID, SESSION_SECRET, WORKER_ID, get_csrf
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from eden_storage import InMemoryStore
from eden_web_ui import make_app

store = InMemoryStore(experiment_id=EXPERIMENT_ID, metrics_schema=_config().metrics_schema)
artifacts_dir = Path('/tmp/eden-review-artifacts')
artifacts_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
app = make_app(
    store=store,
    experiment_id=EXPERIMENT_ID,
    experiment_config=_config(),
    worker_id=WORKER_ID,
    session_secret=SESSION_SECRET,
    claim_ttl_seconds=3600,
    artifacts_dir=artifacts_dir,
    secure_cookies=False,
    now=_now,
)
with TestClient(app) as c:
    c.post('/signin', follow_redirects=False)
    csrf = get_csrf(c)
    r = c.post('/planner/t-missing/add_row', data={'csrf_token': csrf}, headers={'hx-request': 'true'}, follow_redirects=True)
    print(r.status_code)
    print(r.url)
    print('<html' in r.text, '<header' in r.text, 'proposal-rows' in r.text)
    print(r.text[:200].replace('\\n', ' '))
PY"

item.query: 'Submitting a form via htmx has the benefit' in https://htmx.org/docs/

item.text:
**Findings**

1. Medium: the HTMX `add_row` path breaks the no-JS contract on redirect/error branches, because it returns ordinary `303` redirects even for `HX-Request: true`. The button is wired for fragment swaps in [planner_claim.html](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/templates/planner_claim.html:47), and [add_row](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/planner.py:154) returns `303` for missing session/claim at lines [155-164](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/planner.py:155). Per the official [htmx docs](https://htmx.org/docs/) and [HX-Redirect docs](https://htmx.org/headers/hx-redirect/), 3xx response headers are not processed by htmx; the browser follows the redirect and htmx receives the redirected HTML. I reproduced that: an HTMX POST without a claim ended at `/planner/?banner=claim+missing+from+session` and returned a full document (`<html>`, `<header>`), which htmx would then swap into `#proposal-rows`. The no-JS path navigates away; the HTMX path corrupts the draft DOM instead. The new tests in [test_planner_flow.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/tests/test_planner_flow.py:148) only cover the happy-path fragment and miss this branch. This needs an htmx-aware redirect/error response plus tests for missing-claim and signed-out HTMX requests.

2. Low: the updated plan still contradicts itself about why HTMX was chosen. The main tech-stack text says the decisive chunk-1 use case is “add another proposal row” at [docs/plans/eden-phase-9-web-ui.md:66](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-9-web-ui.md:66), but the alternatives section still says HTMX was picked for the “planner-list → claim-form transition” at [docs/plans/eden-phase-9-web-ui.md:159](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-9-web-ui.md:159). That makes the amended rationale less load-bearing than it should be.

Vendoring itself looks clean. The local asset [htmx-1.9.12.min.js](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/static/htmx-1.9.12.min.js:1) hashes to the pinned `449317ade7881e949510db614991e195c3a099c4c791c24dacec55f9f4a2a452`, is 48,101 bytes, contains the standard UMD wrapper, and advertises `version:"1.9.12"`. That matches the existence of the official [v1.9.12 release](https://github.com/bigskysoftware/htmx/releases/tag/v1.9.12) and the official [UNPKG 1.9.12 dist listing](https://app.unpkg.com/htmx.org%401.9.12/files/dist). I did not see tampering indicators. That last step is still an inference from upstream metadata plus file inspection, since I did not find an upstream-published SHA-256 on a primary source.

Everything else I checked was solid: CSRF still runs on the HTMX path in [planner.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/planner.py:157), the claim keying remains per-session via `(session.csrf, task_id)` at [planner.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/planner.py:41), `_proposal_row.html` is a valid fragment target at [_proposal_row.html](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/templates/_proposal_row.html:4), and `hx-swap="beforeend"` against `#proposal-rows` is the right DOM shape. I also ran `uv run pytest -q reference/services/web-ui/tests/test_routes.py reference/services/web-ui/tests/test_security_invariants.py reference/services/web-ui/tests/test_planner_flow.py`; 29 tests passed.

Overall assessment: not clean as-is. I recommend a small follow-up to make HTMX redirects/error exits explicit and test them; after that, the vendoring and happy-path progressive-enhancement wiring look good.
